paragraphs.text: After this, “God said to Jacob: Arise, go up to Bethel… and make there an altar unto God, that appeared unto thee when thou fledst from the face of Esau thy brother. Then Jacob said unto his sons: Put away the strange gods…” which they had taken from plundered Shechem. “And they delivered unto him the strange gods… and the earrings of gold that were in their ears, and they hid them… under the oak” (Gen 35:1–2, 4), that they might be no snare to Jacob’s descendants. “And Jacob came unto Isaac his father… at Hebron” (Gen 35:27) after twenty-three years. “Isaac died, being a hundred fourscore years old; and Esau and Jacob his sons buried him” (Gen 35:29).
